author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXXI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a psalm of David himself, an ecstasy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end a Psalm of David Himself, the Mediator strong of hand in persecutions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the word ecstasy, which is added to the title, signifies a transport of the mind, which is produced either by a panic, or by some revelation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in this Psalm the panic of the people of God troubled by the persecution of all the heathen, and by the failing of faith throughout the world, is principally seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But first the Mediator Himself speaks: then the People redeemed by His Blood gives thanks: at last in trouble it speaks at length, which is what belongs to the ecstasy; but the Person of the Prophet himself is twice interposed, near the end, and at the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thee, O Lord, have I trusted, let Me not be put to confusion for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Thee, O Lord, have I trusted, let Me never be confounded, whilst they shall insult Me as one like other men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy righteousness rescue Me, and deliver Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in Thy righteousness rescue Me from the pit of death, and deliver Me out of their company.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Bend down Thine ear unto Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear Me in My humiliation, nigh at hand unto Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make haste to deliver Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Defer not to the end of the world, as with all who believe on Me, My separation from sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be unto Me a God who protecteth Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be unto Me God, and Protector.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And a house of refuge, that Thou mayest save Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as a house, wherein taking refuge I may be saved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou art My strength, and My refuge” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou art unto Me My strength to bear My persecutors, and My refuge to escape them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And for Thy Name’s sake Thou shalt be My guide, and shalt nourish Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that by Me Thou mayest be known to all the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will in all things follow Thy will; and, by assembling, by degrees, Saints unto Me, Thou shalt fulfil My body, and My perfect stature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt bring Me out of this trap, which they have hidden for Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shalt bring Me out of these snares, which they have hidden for Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou art My Protector.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Into Thy hands I commend My Spirit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To Thy power I commend My Spirit, soon to receive It back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast redeemed Me, O Lord God of truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the people too, redeemed by the Passion of their Lord, and joyful in the glorifying of their Head, say, “Thou hast redeemed me, O Lord God of truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hatest them that hold to vanity uselessly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hatest them that hold to the false happiness of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I have trusted in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will be glad, and rejoice in Thy mercy:” which doth not deceive me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast regarded My humiliation:” wherein Thou hast subjected me to vanity in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast saved my soul from necessities” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast saved my soul from the necessities of fear, that with a free love it may serve Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hast not shut me up into the hands of the enemy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And hast not shut me up, that I should have no opening for recovering unto liberty, and be given over for ever into the power of the devil, ensnaring me with the desire of this life, and terrifying me with death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast set my feet in a large room.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The resurrection of my Lord being known, and mine own being promised me, my love, having been brought out of the straits of fear, walks abroad in continuance, into the expanse of liberty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I am troubled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is this unlooked-for cruelty of the persecutors, striking such dread into me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Have mercy on me, O Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I am now no more alarmed for death, but for torments and tortures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine eye hath been disordered by anger.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I had mine eye upon Thee, that Thou shouldest not abandon me: Thou art angry, and hast disordered it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My soul, and my belly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the same anger my soul hath been disturbed, and my memory, whereby I retained what my God hath suffered for me, and what He hath promised me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my life hath failed in pain” (ver 10).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For my life is to confess Thee, but it failed in pain, when the enemy had said, Let them be tortured until they deny Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my years in groanings.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The time that I pass in this world is not taken away from me by death, but abides, and is spent in groanings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My strength hath been weakened by want.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I want the health of this body, and racking pains come on me: I want the dissolution of the body, and death forbears to come: and in this want my confidence hath been weakened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my bones have been disturbed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And my stedfastness hath been disturbed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been made a reproach above all mine enemies” (ver 11).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the wicked are my enemies; and nevertheless they for their wickednesses are tortured only till they confess: I then have overpassed their reproach, I, whose confession death doth not follow, but racking pains follow upon it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And to my neighbours too much.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hath seemed too much to them, who were already drawing near to know Thee, and to hold the faith that I hold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And a fear to mine acquaintance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And into my very acquaintance I struck fear by the example of my dreadful tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They that did see me, fled without from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they did not understand my inward and invisible hope, they fled from me into things outward and visible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been forgotten, as one dead from the heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they have forgotten me, as if I were dead from their hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have become as a lost vessel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have seemed to myself to be lost to all the Lord’s service, living in this world, and gaining none, when all were afraid to join themselves unto me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I have heard the rebuking of many dwelling by in a circuit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I have heard many rebuking me, in the pilgrimage of this world near me, following the circuit of time, and refusing to return with me to the eternal country.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whilst they were assembling themselves together against me, they conspired that they might take my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That my soul, which should by death easily escape from their power, might consent unto them, they imagined a device, whereby they would not suffer me even to die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I have hoped in Thee, O Lord; I have said, Thou art my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou hast not changed, that Thou shouldest not save, Who dost correct.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy hands” are “my lots” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Thy power are my lots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I see no desert for which out of the universal ungodliness of the human race Thou hast elected me particularly to salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And though there be with Thee some just and secret order in my election, yet I, from whom this is hid, have attained by lot unto my Lord’s vesture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver me from the hands of mine enemies, and from them that persecute me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Make Thy Face to shine upon Thy servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Make it known to men, who do not think that I belong unto Thee, that Thy Face is bent upon me, and that I serve Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save me in Thy mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, let me not be confounded, for I have called upon Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, let me not be put to shame by those who insult me, for that I have called upon Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the ungodly be ashamed, and be brought down to hell.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them rather who call upon stones be ashamed, and made to dwell with darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let the deceitful lips be made dumb” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In making known to the peoples Thy mysteries wrought in me, strike with dumb amazement the lips of them that invent falsehood of me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Which speak iniquity against the Righteous, in pride and contempt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which speak iniquity against Christ, in their pride and contempt of Him as a crucified man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How great” is “the multitude of Thy sweetness, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here the Prophet exclaims, having sight of all this, and admiring how manifoldly plenteous is Thy sweetness, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Which Thou hast hid for them that fear Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even those, whom Thou correctest, Thou lovest much: but lest they should go on negligently from relaxed security, Thou hidest from them the sweetness of Thy love, for whom it is profitable to fear Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast perfected it for them that hope in Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Thou hast perfected this sweetness for them that hope in Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou dost not withdraw from them what they look for perseveringly even unto the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In sight of the sons of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it does not escape the notice of the sons of men, who now live no more after Adam, but after the Son of Man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt hide them in the hidden place of Thy Countenance:” which seat Thou shalt preserve for everlasting in the hidden place of the knowledge of Thee for them that hope in Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the troubling of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that now they suffer no more trouble from men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt protect them in Thy tabernacle from the contradiction of tongues” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But here meanwhile whilst evil tongues murmur against them, saying, Who hath come thence?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt protect them in the tabernacle, that of faith in those things, which the Lord wrought and endured for us in time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed be the Lord; for He hath made His mercy marvellous, in the city of compassing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessed be the Lord, for after the correction of the sharpest persecutions He hath made His mercy marvellous to all throughout the world, in the circuit of human society.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I said in my ecstasy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence that people again speaking saith, I said in my fear, when the heathen were raging horribly against me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been cast forth from the sight of Thine eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if Thou hadst regard to me, Thou wouldest not suffer me to endure these things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore Thou heardest, O Lord, the voice of my prayer, when I cried unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore putting a limit to correction, and showing that I have part in Thy care, Thou heardest, O Lord, the voice of my prayer, when I raised it high out of tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Love the Lord, all ye His saints” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Prophet again exhorts, having sight of these things, and saith, “Love the Lord, all ye His saints; for the Lord will require truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since “if the righteous shall scarcely be saved, where shall the sinner and the ungodly appear?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He will repay them that do exceeding proudly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He will repay them who even when conquered are not converted, because they are very proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Quit you like men, and let your heart be strengthened” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 24): working good without fainting, that ye may reap in due season.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All ye who trust in the Lord:” that is, ye who duly fear and worship Him, trust ye in the Lord.
